page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i5ge.12xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at 3.2 GHz. The instance features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs, utilizing a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 288.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.0 GB of RAM per core. The hardware configuration includes 7679 GB of local SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 84 Gbps, running on a KVM hypervisor.

The inclusion of 7679 GB of local SSD storage directly within the instance profile offers high resource density, eliminating the need for external block storage attachments for primary data operations. This bundled storage design provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-capacity local data access. However, the single thread per core design means there is no hyperthreading, which limits concurrent thread execution compared to multi-threaded architectures but ensures dedicated execution resources per vCPU.

Given the combination of 48 dedicated cores, 288.0 GB of RAM, and substantial local SSD capacity, this instance is configured for storage-intensive and database workloads. It is suitable for hosting relational databases, NoSQL databases, data warehousing, and localized data processing tasks that require high-speed access to large datasets and high network throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i5ge.12xlarge is an x86_64 server instance featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C physical cores running at 3.2 GHz with a single thread per core. It is configured with 288.0 GB of RAM, providing 6.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 7679 GB of local SSD storage. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 84 Gbps. The integration of high-capacity local SSD storage and dedicated CPU resources makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for data-dense environments by reducing reliance on external block storage. It is designed for database hosting, data warehousing, and storage-intensive processing workloads. The single thread per core design ensures dedicated execution resources without hyperthreading overhead, while the KVM hypervisor manages virtualization.
